rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN J. REILLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The Court waives the following defects: (1) the first amendment showed the incorrect statutory compensation in paragraph 3; (2) the net amount of cash alleged in the second amendment, meant to amend paragraph 35(b) of the petition, has two different figures; (3) the proof of service filed on 11/20/24 shows the incorrect or incomplete captions of the First Amendment and the Second Amendment in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing, and item 2 of the proof of service was incomplete (address of the person who served notice); (4) the attorney time sheets do not support the extraordinary fee categories, as time entries are merely shown chronologically, without subtotals for each category or even a total of all the hours and amounts, as claimed in the fee declaration filed on 10/4/24 and the [Corrected] Third Supplement filed on 11/19/24; (5) the total attorney hours and total extraordinary fees do not appear to be correct in either the attorney's fee declaration filed on 10/4/24 or in the [Corrected] Third Supplement filed on 11/19/24; however, no one has objected to the request for extraordinary attorney fees; and (6) the amended prayer in the second amendment filed on 11/15/24 does not include the executor's request to reimburse himself $28,000.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD CORNELIUS COUGHLIN III
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/28/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of the notice of the hearing of the PETITION has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is filed (or e-filed, if e-filing). (4) The proof of service filed on 11/25/24 was for just the "verified first supplement," not also of the PETITION, did not have a printout of the remote appearance instructions attached to the notice, and shows that notice was mailed on 11/22/24, before the supplement was filed on 11/25/24. (5) The creditor, whose claim is rejected, has 90 days to act on the rejected claim. (It is unclear that the Dept. of Tax and Fee Administration is subject to the time limitations on filing a creditor's claim.) (6) There is no Exhibit A attached to the supplement filed on 11/25/24. (7) Under what authority did the administrator close the business and "reopen" it as her own? (8) How much was the former attorney for the administrator paid? He will likely be required to appear at a future hearing, as there is no authority for payment of fees to an attorney without a Court order. [CRC 7.700(a).] Deadline is 1/14/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY NANG TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 07/05/2024 and posted as tentative rulings for previous two hearings) since this petition was initially set for 07/16/2024: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified by the petitioner. 4) Paragraph 7 of the petition: i) An allegation must also be made as to whether any heir HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). ii) The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 5) Paragraph 10: Was notice of each proposed action given to the probate referee who requested special notice? 6) Paragraph 15: No summary of account was attached to the petition. 7) Paragraph 18: No Attachment 1 (schedule of proposed distribution) was attached to the petition. 8) Paragraph 19: No assignment has been filed as to Jacob Swaim's interest. 9) Paragraph 20 & Schedule A: If the $2,899.78 payment is a partial or full refund of selling expenses deducted from the proceeds through escrow, the statutory fee base receipts must be reduced by $2,899.78. 10) Paragraph 24: No declaration itemizing the costs sought to be reimbursed has been filed. 11) No schedule of disbursements was attached to the petition. 12) No schedule of property on hand at close of account was attached to the petition. 13) Financial account statements must be filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of original statements required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AJAY DRALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, as there was no objection to the request for extraordinary fees, even though the time spent on ordinary services (summarized in the attorney's supplement e-filed on 12/6/24) did not show detailed time entries for the Court to determine if even that time was truly for ordinary services, over $4,000 was billed just to prepare the fee declaration, the supplement was not also signed or verified by the Court-appointed fiduciary, as required by CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023, and the attorney costs were not itemized.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY VAN WAMBEKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1. Notice of the most recent amendment was served on November 9, 2024, prior to the amendment having been filed. Notice may not be mailed, served, or published before the filing of the pleading requiring notice (LRSF 14.14.A). 2. Sums remaining on deposit at the death of a party to a joint account belong to the surviving party or parties as against the estate of the decedent unless there is clear and convincing evidence of a different intent (Probate Code Section 5302(a)). Court to determine whether the cash is property of the estate. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONG YEE CHIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the proof of service of notice field on 11/18/24 did not show that an AMENDMENT had been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] The amendment was not e-filed until 11/19/24, so that even if notice had shown that an amendment had been filed, mailing notice on 11/18/24 would have been defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Instructions for how to appear remotely were also attached to a "supplement," when they should have been attached to the Notice of Hearing of the petition and amendment. The instructions also had several typos in paragraph A.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONNA THIEMECKE ALTICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will and codicil, both dated 3/4/24. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 12/23/2025. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 12/23/2025, no appearance will be required on that date. (NB: The proposed executor did not mail notice, as alleged in the supplement filed on 11/18/24. A disinterested party must (and did) serve notice. Notice was additionally sent to Jonathan Bartunek at an address other than the one shown to be his on Attachment 8 and without any explanation.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH ANN WIMBERLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (3) Item 7 of the petition must be answered when a decedent is not survived by spouse or issue. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents, grandparents, aunts/uncles and when did they die? Deadline is 12/24/24 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (This Court no longer requires the declaration of real property.)
